rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in the component type scope and implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope
Use valid component type scope for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when adding implementation-level bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on whether they refer to component ports or implementation details
Use valid visible identifiers in AGREE expressions to maintain traceability and avoid unresolved references
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for assumptions and guarantees
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equations and `const name : type = value;` for constants
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Avoid unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers in AGREE expressions are valid and visible
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
